entry.title: Simplified Transportation Document: Two New CT-e Types
entry.content:
Versão em português

Hi, community!

The Brazilian tax authority, SEFAZ, has recently issued a technical note introducing the Simplified CT-e. This new layout, while still using model 57, is designed to streamline B2C and retail processes.

When to Use the Simplified CT-e

The Simplified CT-e comes into play when there are multiple goods senders or destinations, but the service taker and type of operation remain the same. It's important to note that the same vehicle and trip must be used for all transports.

Changes in the XML Layout

The tpCTe field now has two new values: Simplified CT-e and Simplified CT-e Replacement.

System Updates

These changes are reflected in your system with new domain values added to CT-e Type Field in the Nota Fiscal type (J_1BAA) Table. However, the values in the Domain are not the same in XML. This mapping is handled in CT-e routines where they match the code expected by Sefaz, so new values are not created as 4 and 5, but as P and R instead.

Relevant SAP Notes for this legal requirement

You can install the following SAP Note to update your solution:

  • SAP Note 3498154 - NT 2024.002 - v.1.04: Incoming Simplified CT-e

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find if a solution is planned for a legal change. For the Technical Note 2024.002 - v.1.04, check CT-e Simplificado - simplified transportation document. Take a look at the SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the Regulatory Change Manager page to understand how to use this tool.

In conclusion, the Simplified CT-e is designed to simplify processes and improve efficiency. As always, we're here to help you navigate these changes and ensure a smooth transition.

If you enjoyed reading this post, feel free to share it with your colleagues. I invite you to follow my profile and leave a comment or a question. Remember to follow the SAP S/4HANA Logistics for Brazil tag to get the latest news available.

All the best,

Eduardo

entry.title: Transport Status List Tool - S/4HANA Edition
entry.content:
Transport Status List

This tool provides You a list of Transport Requests of Your team or the whole system. Helps people dealing with release management.

Supported onPremise versions: 2022+

Filter capabilities

  • Request owner
  • Request type: Workbench, Customizing, Transport of Copy etc.
  • CTS Project
  • Release status
  • Systems of your landscape: Development, Quality, Pre-production, Production. Best to save it in a variant. Entering the Pre-Production System ID parameter will show additional columns with the Import Status

Working with the ALV

Actions and Hotspots

  • Display Log of the selected TR
  • Free text search in text columns ( currently enabled for Short Description)
  • Double click on TR Number results in displaying technical field names/values
  • Click on Short description opens the TR

Technicals

The report itself is based on CDS Entities created for this purpose, due such CDS objects are not present in the SAP standard on top of the transport request tables. As result it provides fast HANA based search capabilities comparing to the legacy version. The import status/return code of the transport requests are however still read from the target systems using RFC enabled function modules. You do not need to do any config for RFC, because the application is using the default TMS configuration.

Planned features:

  • Customizing to store system landscape configuration
  • Fiori List report

Installation

Pull the onPrem-2022 branch (which is the default now) using abapGit.

https://github.com/attilaberencsi/trlist.git

Enjoy 😉

Note: the old version (main branch) is not supported anymore.

entry.title: Logistics Collaboration – Eyes, Hands, and Feet
entry.content:
Transportation planners are often like the eyes-in-the-sky, overseeing transportation and logistics activity around the world in real-time.  But the logistics practitioners that keep our supply chains going know that while logistics visibility is essential, getting goods from point A to point B takes hands and feet too.  There is leg work involved (even if automated) in the physical movement of good, and there is no getting around it.  So, while partners along a supply chain strive for efficiency, savings, and customer satisfaction, risk of disruptions affecting the leg work along a route still persists. 


Luckily, data along a logistics network moves faster than the physical movement of goods.  So with the right tools, supply chain professionals can plan around the risks and react to mitigate impact when there is a disruption.

A different type of leg work, but let me do some of the legwork for you to learn more about SAP’s logistics collaboration solutions that help companies and their logistics partners gain efficiency and visibility to improve their supply chain resilience.  

Check out these recent and upcoming SAP Business Network logistics collaboration stories, events, and articles below, and leave a comment to share your questions or feedback.

SAP Business Network for Logistics Customer Stories

Organización Corona: Creating supply chain visibility to increase logistics efficiency and sustainability

Corona presented at two breakout sessions at SAP Sapphire this year, sharing how their SAP logistics solution helps them routinely achieve 98% of third-party carriers on the network available within 24 hours.

Celsa and SAP partner Westernacher: Transforming steel logistics with SAP Transportation Management and Business Network for Logistics

Hear directly from the Supply Chain Manager, Global Logistics Manager, and transportation planners are international steel producer Celsa in this video, how they saved 1-2% of their transportation costs using SAP’s supply chain logistics solutions, with expert implementation support from SAP partner Westernacher.  

Webinars

The Expanding Role of Visibility, Efficiency & Compliance in the Connected Supply Chain

Register for this upcoming webinar on September 9th, to hear SAP Business Network for Logistics customer Al Dahra share experience and insights on why global supply chains need cross-company logistics network solutions to deliver better visibility, efficiency, and compliance. 

Inbound Logistics Visibility with SAP and LeverX

Catch this webinar on-demand to see a concise demonstration of how inbound logistics visibility enables insight-to-action that links logistics execution with procurement and planning. 

Podcast

The Future of Supply Chain, Episode 72: The Power of Real-Time Visibility in Supply Chains with SAP and project44

Timeliness, accuracy, and completeness – three key pillars of differentiation in logistics visibility.  Hear project44’s Chris Kobus and SAP’s Doug DeLuca discuss just how these three key aspects of logistics visibility are critical to efficiency and performance.

Article

Inbound Logistics Magazine: Increasing Efficiency with Third-Party Transportation

SAP’s August solution release brought a native network convergence innovation linking processes among, buyers, suppliers, and logistics service providers through the SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration and Freight Collaboration solutions.  Read this article for the business case behind the innovation.  

 

Learn more about SAP’s supply chain logistics solutions here:

https://www.sap.com/products/business-network/solutions.html#logistics-collaboration

https://www.sap.com/products/scm/supply-chain-logistics.html

entry.title: What's new in SAP Business Network for Supply Chain - Release 2408
entry.content:
SAP Business Network for Supply Chain 

Welcome to our latest blog post on "What’s New in SAP Business Network for Supply Chain" for 2024. This entry will specifically cover the top 3 features introduced in the Q3 2024 Release. It is designed for those seeking a detailed overview of the enhancements launched in August 2024 for SAP Business Network for Supply Chain. Below, we provide a summary of the updates anticipated to benefit our customers.

 

1. Improving Supply Chain Resiliency with End-to-end Shipment Visibility

Enables buyers and suppliers on the SAP Business Network for Supply Chain to work together with logistics service providers for the outsourced transportation planning of buyer-managed shipments. The freight forwarder handles the transportation planning for inbound shipments on behalf of the buyer, using the supplier's advanced ship notice.

Some of the supply chain visibility challenges that trading partners are currently experiencing are:

  • Gaps in order-to-stock process visibility when buyers subcontract transportation to 3PLs
  • Disconnected direct procurement and transportation processes
  • Lack of timely updates impacts buyers and suppliers’ ability to identify and address potential issues

As part of Q3 2024 release, the integration between SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration and SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration has been introduced to support the Buyer-managed transportation planning outsourced to Logistics Service Providers capability.

Through this new capability, buyers can now initiate the transportation planning by sharing the advanced ship notice from their suppliers with their logistics service providers. The logistics service provider shares the planned shipment with the buyer along with updates to the advanced ship notice.

In this way, buyers automatically get updates when their purchase is scheduled to arrive including updates on potential changes to the arrival date. Buyers can plan their warehousing, production, and customer expectations more accurately.

The following values are updated in the Ship Notice generated by the supplier in SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration based on the Shipment plan information received from SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration: Bill Of Lading, Carrier name, Delivery Date, Service Level and Transit Direction. A Ship Notice update is sent to the buyer system including these modified values.

Also, a new “Shipment Detail” tab has been added inside the Ship Notice document which contains all the Shipment plan information provided by the Carrier and sent to Supply Chain Collaboration from Freight Collaboration.

Ship Notices list is now available as part of the new Ship Notices Workbench tile:

  • Buyers and Suppliers can now filter by Shipping and Delivery date
  • Users can search for ASNs created in the Last 365 days
  • Ship Notices forwarded to Business Network for Freight Collaboration can be easily identified.
  • Latest Shipment Planning status shown

Benefits provided by this new capability:

  • Buyers and suppliers regain visibility through this integrated process that provide transparency to delivery times and goods receipt expectations
  • Greater visibility and timely updates provided to buyers, suppliers, and 3PLs throughout the order-to-stock process enable early identification and resolution of potential issues

 

2. Enhancements in component consumption including additional validation

Improving subcontracting processes by enabling the tracking and validation of the lifecycle of finished products and their components. Suppliers can now provide customer batch information for batch-managed components when creating component consumption in Ship Notices or reporting component consumption in subcontracting scenarios.

Here are some key points to highlight:

  • cXML enhancement: Version 1.2.060 of cXML for OrderRequest now includes an update to transmit batch-required information for subcontracting components.
  • New transaction rule: A new rule has been introduced to enable verification for batch-managed components, ensuring accuracy and compliance.
  • Error message: Error messages will be displayed if the customer batch number is not provided for components during component consumption reporting in the UI. This applies both during the creation of a ship notice and in general component consumption reporting, enhancing data integrity.
  • New button is available now: “Copy Remaining Quantities”. It will enable users to automatically populate the remaining quantity of components in a purchase order based on the components consumed so far for the PO item, thereby streamlining the process, and enhancing efficiency. This functionality is available both during the creation of ship notices and when reporting consumption.

 

3. Migration of Business Rules from the Test Environment to the Production System

We are pleased to introduce a new functionality that enable buyer administrators to efficiently manage default transaction rules and supplier group transaction rules within their SAP Business Network buyer accounts. This feature allows for the seamless download and upload of transaction rules in a spreadsheet format, facilitating the transfer of rules between different environments or for archival purposes.

Key Benefits:

  • Effortless Migration: Easily export transaction rules from a test environment and import them into a production environment. This ensures consistency and accuracy when transitioning from testing to live operations.
  • Comprehensive Download Options: When downloading default transaction rules, all rules are consolidated into a single spreadsheet. For supplier group rules, administrators can selectively download rules for individual supplier groups configured in their environment.
  • Multilingual Support: Transaction rules can be translated into any supported language within the SAP Business Network product capabilities during the download process, ensuring global applicability and ease of use.
  • Accessible File Management: Downloaded transaction rules are conveniently available under the Downloads tab on the Manage Transaction Rules Details page. Similarly, uploaded files can be accessed under the Uploads tab, providing a streamlined and organized approach to file management.

Export default transaction rules

Import default transaction rules

Export supplier group rules

 

This feature is particularly useful for scenarios where transaction rules are tested in a non-production environment and need to be replicated in the production environment. By downloading the rules from the test environment and uploading them to the production environment, administrators can ensure a smooth and error-free transition.

We believe this enhancement will significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of managing transaction rules, ultimately contributing to better operational performance and compliance.

 

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

These are the key features for Q3 2024. Please review the readiness content for comprehensive details on these key features for Q3 2024.

For a complete overview of the Q3 2024 release features for SAP Business Network for Supply Chain, please refer to the following resources: link

Please visit https://www.sap.com/businessnetwork  to learn more about SAP Business Network.

To explore SAP Business Network for Supply Chain, visit SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration

For updates on our community and activities, please visit the: SAP Business Network Community

For the IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Multi-Enterprise Supply Chain Commerce Network 2023 Vendor Assessment, please review the following link: IDC MarketScape

SAP Managed Tags:

SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration, 

SAP Business Network Planning Collaboration

Previous release blogs:

SAP Business Network 2311 Release – What’s new?

What's new in SAP Business Network for Supply Chain - Release 2402

What's new in SAP Business Network for Supply Chain - Release 2405

entry.title: When and How to contact the SAP ID Service team?
entry.content:
You are logging into a SAP site but your attempt was unsuccessful? Don’t worry, the SAP ID Service team is ready to assist! Read on to learn more on the various topics this team supports, the team contact details as well as a few trouble shooting tips along the way.

So, let’s get started!

Firstly, a quick description of the SAP ID Service team. This team manages the login process for any accounts.sap.com login issues. This includes logins to all SAP sites with a S-user ID or P-user ID.

 You can reach out to the SAP ID Service team for assistance on:

Password reset issues: An email triggered by the password reset request has not arrived in your email inbox or you are receiving an error.

Troubleshooting Tips:

  • S-user ID password reset: You can try resetting your S-user password here by following the steps in KBA 1808560 - How to reset an S-user ID password - SAP ID Service - SAP for Me.
  • P-user ID password reset: You can try resetting your P-user ID password here by following the steps in KBA 3250797 - How to reset a P-user ID password - SAP for Me

 You are receiving an error message: The error message may contain the following for example: Verify Your Email, Blocked users, not being an existing user, etc.

Troubleshooting Tip:

  • S-user ID: 2371719 - S-user ID login error: Sorry, we could not authenticate you. Try again. - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

There is an issue with the Multi-factor authentication (MFA): The time-based one-time password (TOTP) MFA is not functioning when accessing a SAP website and an error message appears.

Troubleshooting Tip:

  • You can follow the steps to Disable and Re-enable MFA Through Your SAP Account. MFA (sap.com) to see if this solves your issue.

You have an issue with Login security settings: This could be related to invalid password attempts that locks your user ID etc.

You have a P-user ID and wish to change your email address: The SAP ID Service team is the main point of contact for all P-user inquiries/issues, including changing contact details or e-mail address: 3302836 - How to change a P-user ID's contact details and email address - SAP for Me

You want to delete your P-user ID: If for whatever reason you no longer have use for your P-user ID, you can make a request for deletion to the SAP ID Service team:3120548 - How to delete a Public user ID (P-user ID) - SAP for Me You will see more information on P-user ID's here: 2482020 - Logon issues due to a P-user ID - SAP for Me

You have a Partner self-registered S-user and need to reset your P-user ID password: Your S-user ID is a Partner self-registered S-user, created from the SAP Partner Portal. You must use the associated P-user ID / e-mail for password reset requests as the P-user is the main user. You can make a request for password reset to the SAP ID Service team: 3049234 - Partner self-registered S-users: Login and password reset issues - SAP for Me

You have a cloud login issue: Many cloud portals/products also use the SAP ID Service and the URL accounts.sap.com to check the login data. You can either reach out to the SAP ID Service team or contact the responsible cloud options team listed in KBA 3053848 - Directory of SAP Cloud Products and Component Areas (Who to Contact) - SAP for Me

So now you know the issues that the SAP ID Service team can assist with.

Next let’s look at how to get in contact with the team: 

You can email the SAP ID Service team directly at sso@sap.com.

Please be sure to include full details of the issue (e.g. S-user ID, P-user ID, application URL, errors, screenshots, etc). This will help the team to provide a speedy resolution.

If you are looking to change the contact details or email address of your P-user ID or delete your P-user ID, send the email from the email address used for the P-User creation and include a screenshot of the P-user ID. You will see more details on P-User ID’s here: Information About Public User ID - SAP Community

--------------

Please see this related KBA which you may find helpful:

3053832 - How to contact SAP ID Service team for password and login help for all SAP sites - SAP for Me

--------------

Thanks for reading!   

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please ask a questions in the Q & A using All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

Read more #CustomerInteractionCenter blog-posts here!

entry.title: Gen AI meets SAP: Improve your sales strategies with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere and SAC
entry.content:
Introduction

In my previous blog , How Metadata Enrichment can enhance SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine on GenAI scenarios , i focused on leveraging the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine together with Metadata Enrichment through GenAI, to further extend analytical insights. This blog will focus on combining sales data from SAP S/4HANA with product reviews, centrally stored in SAP Datasphere. Leveraging the powerful analytics capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), we are will generate actionable sales insights that uplift the conventional sales strategies.

This blog  will explore the benefits of combing sentiment analysis (using SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Large Language Models [LLM])  and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). These cutting-edge technologies together enhance sales and market strategies. These tools, can help businesses transition from basic descriptive analyses to advanced predictive and prescriptive insights, thereby unlocking  significant market opportunities. We'll examine how sentiment analysis reveals customer sentiment from product reviews, while LLMs and SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine facilitate the processing and contextualization of vast data sets. Integration solutions,part of SAP BTP, ensure seamless data flow and scalability, as well as real-time responsiveness. 

Integrating Sales Data with Product Review Sentiments

The first step is to integrate sales data from SAP S/4HANA into SAP Datasphere. At the same time, we will take advantage of Generative AI (Gen AI) to analyze product reviews, applying sentiment analysis to understand customer opinions and trends. These reviews, enriched with sentiment scores and categorized against segments of interest (such as product, supplier or delivery experience), are then ingested into SAP Datasphere. This approach harmonizes structured and unstructured data; and also prepares the data for advanced, multi-dimensional analytics.

By combining transactional data with customer sentiments, businesses can gain valuable insights using SAP Analytics Cloud. This integration allows companies to use years of logged data to identify areas for improvement such as enhancing customer support and optimizing delivery services. This data-driven approach helps businesses shift from reactive decision-making to proactive strategies that meet market needs and improve customer satisfaction.

Leveraging Generative AI for Sentiment Analysis of Product Reviews ( Using Synthetic Data )

To conduct this analysis, we used an approach similar to the one in the previous blog. However, in this study, we created synthetic data that closely matches the characteristics of the original dataset. Using synthetic data is important because it allows us to test and train our models without compromising the privacy and integrity of the actual data. Nowadays, generating synthetic data is quite simple with generative AI. In order to do this, each product review was converted into a vector on SAP HANA Cloud within SAP Datasphere. We enriched these vectors with the sentiments and information found in the original dataset.

We used Gen AI to:

  • Create reviews that have the same overall sentiment as the original ones.
  • Mention similar issues encountered, such as product delivery and price sensitivity.
  • Address other relevant factors by considering them at different hierarchical levels.

This approach ensures that our synthetic data is both secure and highly functional for analysis

Synthetic dataset regarding product reviews:

  • User's Overall Review (negative, positive, neutral)
  • Delivery Review (On Time, Delayed, Never Delivered ...) 
  • Price Review (Value for Money, Overpriced ...)
  • Supplier Review (Satisfied, Very Disappointed ...)
  • Customer Support (Satisfied, Very Disappointed ...) 
  • Issues Encountered (Breakouts, Missing Brush ...)
  • Issues Resolutions (Price decrease, Product Refund ...)
  • Resolution Level 1 (Delivery, Packaging ...)
  • Resolution Level 2 (Accessories, Leakage ...)
  • Resolution Level 3 (Color Difference, Expiration Date ...)

    The information concerning the issues of resolutions and their resolving levels was too extensive. An additional pass through using GenAI was necessary to organize this data in a more meaningful manner. Additionally, four more summarized pieces of information regarding the reviews were added :

    • Resolution (Improved Packaging, Improved Pricing ...)
    • Level 1 (Customer Support, Safety Concerns ...)
    • Level 2 (Design Issues, Missing Product ...)
    • Level 3 (Broken Parts, Product Quantity ...)

For example, analyzing two product reviews, the following metadata has been extracted:

 

Supplementary Data Sources and Final Integration

Taking into consideration that this is a demo, we limited the available attributes per "Product", "Customer" and "Supplier" entities. The same constraint apply to the Sales Value and Sales Volume metric. By merging sales data from SAP S/4HANA with sentiment analysis within SAP Datasphere, we developed the final analytical model which will be consumed on SAP Analytics Cloud.

The lineage analysis from the SAP Datasphere model is the following:

This model includes the following dimensions: Product, Calendar, Customer and Supplier along with Sales Data.

Transforming Sales Strategy with SAP Analytics Cloud

By leveraging SAC’s advanced analytical capabilities, businesses can now perform multi-faceted analyses to discover hidden patterns and trends on top of the diversified Data Sources.

Sales teams can drill down into regional customer satisfaction and correlate it with supplier efficiency to identify market-specific strategies. Additionally, predictive analytics powered by SAC can forecast future sales trends by analyzing historical data and enriched generative AI insights, allowing for proactive inventory management and targeted marketing campaigns.Moreover, Reviews outlining customer issues may yield insights for further improvement, e.g. improvement in logistics and delivery services.

The synergy between SAC and Gen AI thus transforms static sales reports into a living strategy dashboard. This powerful combination enables a more informed, responsive, and strategic sales approach, strengthening the business’s competitive position in the market.

Introducing the GenAI-Powered SAP Analytic Cloud Dashboard

    1. Sales Overview

This dashboard offers a comprehensive overview of sales data, which can be utilized by different personas within a business to make well-informed decisions. Here are some of the business scenarios, which highlight how various roles can leverage these graphs.

Scenario 1: Sales Performance Optimization

Persona: Sales manager

Objective: Monitor and enhance sales performance.

Analytical Insights:

  • Total sales value, volume, and products: The sales manager can quickly assess the overall sales performance by looking at the total sales value (260,000.00), total volume (3,871), and total products (1,810). This provides a snapshot of the business health.
  • Sales per category and subcategory: By analyzing the sales per category and subcategory, the manager can identify which product lines are performing well and which are underperforming. For instance, "Ready and Cream" appears to be a significant contributor.
  • Top and bottom performing products: This helps in identifying the best-selling products like "Skin Renew" and the least performing ones like "Skin Prep." The manager can then strategize to promote the bottom products or phase them out.

Scenario 2: Customer Segmentation and Targeting

Persona: Marketing team

Objective: Tailor marketing campaigns based on customer demographics.

Actionable Insights:

  • Average sales per customer age group: The marketing team can use this data to understand which age groups are contributing most to sales. For example, the age group 35-44 has the highest average sales. Gen AI can create personalized marketing messages and campaigns targeting specific age groups based on their purchasing behavior. For instance, if Gen AI identifies that the 35-44 age group prefers "Skin Renew," targeted ads can be created for this demographic.
  • Total Sales and Volume per Year/Month: This helps in identifying seasonal trends and planning marketing campaigns accordingly.

Scenario 3: Supplier Relationship Management

Persona: Supply chain manager

Objective: Manage and optimize supplier relationships.

Actionable Insights:

  • Total suppliers and average supplier sentiment per category: The supply chain manager can monitor the performance and sentiment of suppliers. For instance, categories like "Bodycare" and "Skincare" have high sentiment scores, indicating good supplier relationships.
  • Sales per product subcategory: This helps in understanding which subcategories are driving sales and ensuring that suppliers for these subcategories are managed effectively. Gen AI can automate communication with suppliers, ensuring timely updates and addressing any issues promptly. This can improve efficiency and maintain strong supplier relationships.

Scenario 4: Product Development and Innovation

Persona: Product development team

Objective: Innovate and develop new products based on market demand.

Actionable Insights:

  • Top and bottom performing products: By analyzing the top-performing products, the product development team can identify features and attributes that are popular among customers.
  • Sales per category and subcategory: This helps in identifying market trends and gaps, guiding the development of new products that meet customer needs.

Scenario 5: Customer Relationship Management

Persona: Customer relationship manager

Objective: Enhance customer satisfaction and loyalty.

Actionable Insights:

  • Total customers and average sales per customer age group: The customer relationship manager can identify key customer segments and tailor engagement strategies accordingly.
  • Sales per product subcategory: Understanding which products are popular among different customer segments can drive personalized customer interactions and recommendations.

    2. Sales / Sentiment Analysis

 

This dashboard provides a detailed overview of sales and sentiment analysis, segmented by various categories, subcategories, suppliers, and time periods. With user-friendly filters and input controls, it allows users to dive deep into the data based on specific categories, subcategories, suppliers, and time frames. Sentiment analysis is visualized through pie charts that represent customer feelings on price, support, delivery, product, and supplier.

Scenario 1: Product Management and Marketing

Persona: Product or marketing manager

Objective: Identify which products are performing well and which are not. For instance, the "Facecare" product in the "Skin Renew" subcategory has the highest sales ($9,195.00) and a high product rating (4.5). This indicates a successful product that could be further promoted.

Scenario 2: Customer Support Improvement

Persona: Customer support manager

Objective: Sentiment analysis identifies areas that need improvement. The "Customer Support Sentiment" pie chart shows that 46.20% of customers have a negative or very negative sentiment towards support. This is a critical area for improvement in support.

Scenario 3: Supplier Relationship Management

Persona: Supply chain manager

Objective: "Customer Supplier Sentiment" data can be used to evaluate supplier performance. With 52.93% of customers satisfied or very satisfied with suppliers, there is room for improvement.

Scenario 4: Strategic Planning and Forecasting

Persona: Business analyst, strategic planner

Objective: Use the sales trends over time to forecast future sales and plan inventory accordingly. The line graph shows fluctuating sales with peaks around specific dates, indicating potential seasonal trends.

Actionable Insights:

  • Product Rating and Sales: The bar chart shows that "Facecare" and "Shampoo & Conditioner" have high sales and ratings, indicating strong market performance. on the contrary, "Anti-Aging" has lower sales, suggesting a need for marketing efforts or product improvement.
  • Customer Sentiments: The pie charts reveal that while price and delivery sentiments are largely positive, customer support and product sentiments have significant negative feedback. This highlights areas for immediate attention.
  • Product Reviews: The bar chart indicates that "Beauty Tools & Devices" and "Skincare" have the highest volume of reviews, both positive and negative. This suggests these categories are highly engaging but may also have quality issues that need addressing.
  • Customer Demographics: The table shows that the 45-55 age group has the highest sales volume ($75,149.00) and generally positive ratings across all metrics. This demographic could be targeted for loyalty programs or personalized marketing campaigns.

    3. Sales and Sentiment Geo Analysis Overview

This dashboard provides analytical insights, including customer support ratings, price ratings, delivery ratings, supplier ratings, a word cloud of top keywords from product reviews, and a geographical sales distribution map.

Scenario 1: Customer Experience Enhancement

Persona: Customer experience manager

Objective: The customer experience manager can use the dashboard to monitor and improve customer satisfaction.

Actionable Insights:

  • If the delivery rating is low, the manager can investigate logistics and supply chain issues.
  • If the price rating is low, they can consider revising pricing strategies or offering promotions.
  • By analyzing the customer support rating, price rating, delivery rating, and supplier rating, they can identify areas needing improvement.

Scenario 2. Market Expansion Strategy

Persona: Marketing manager

Objective: Use the geographical sales distribution map to identify regions with high and low sales performance.

Actionable Insights:

  • If sales are high in Northern Europe but low in Southern Europe, the manager can allocate more marketing resources to Southern Europe and tailor campaigns to local tastes.
  • Use the word cloud to identify popular product features and emphasize them in marketing materials.

Scenario 3: Product Development and Improvement

Persona: Product manager

Objective: Use the word cloud and customer ratings to gather feedback on product performance and identify areas for improvement.

Actionable Insights:

  • If customers frequently mention "razor" and "blade" in a positive context, the manager can consider expanding the product line to include more shaving products.
  • If "smell" and "oil" are mentioned negatively, they can work on improving the product formulation.
  • if "skin" and "hair" are frequently mentioned, the manager can focus on these aspects in product development.

Scenario 4. Supply Chain Optimization

Persona: Supply chain manager

Objective: Use the delivery rating and geographical sales data to optimize logistics and supply chain operations.

Actionable Insights:

  • If delivery ratings are low in certain regions, the manager can investigate and address bottlenecks in the supply chain.
  • Use sales data to ensure that high-demand regions are adequately stocked.

    4. Detailed Analysis on Issues

Users can explore the complex relationships between product reviews and their corresponding resolution outcomes, segmented by resolution levels ranging from 1 to 3. This tab offers a thorough analysis of how effectively customer issues and reviews have been addressed, providing a detailed view of resolution efficacy. By filtering data based on specific suppliers or products, users can identify patterns and correlations that may affect customer satisfaction and product performance.

Scenario 1: Supplier Performance Analysis

Persona: Supply chain manager

Objective: Identify and address supplier-related issues to improve overall supply chain efficiency.

Actionable Insights:

  • Bar Chart Analysis: The "Nbr of Resolution per Supplier (Top 10)" chart shows that "GlamGlow Goods Delivery" has the highest number of resolutions (287), followed by "PoshParcel" (284) and "BeautyBeam Carriers" (278). This indicates that these suppliers are frequently involved in issues that require resolution.
  • Flow Diagram Analysis: By examining the flow diagram, the Supply Chain Manager can trace specific issues back to these suppliers. For instance, if "GlamGlow Goods Delivery" is frequently associated with "Packaging Problems" or "Delivery Delays," these insights can be used to negotiate better terms or seek alternative suppliers.

Scenario 2: Product Quality Improvement

Persona: Product manager

Objective: Enhance product quality and customer satisfaction by addressing common product-related issues.

Actionable Insights:

  • Bar Chart Analysis: The "Nbr of Resolution per Product (Top 10)" chart highlights that "Skin Revive" and "Hair Revive" have the highest number of resolutions (48 and 41 respectively). This suggests that these products are frequently problematic.
  • Flow Diagram Analysis: The Product Manager can use the flow diagram to identify specific issues associated with these products. For example, "Skin Revive" might be linked to "Product Quality Issues" or "Application Difficulties."

Scenario 3: Customer Support Optimization

Persona: Customer support manager

Objective: Streamline customer support processes and improve resolution times.

Actionable Insights:

  • Bar Chart Analysis: The charts provide insights into which products and suppliers are generating the most issues, allowing the Customer Support Manager to allocate resources more effectively.
  • Flow Diagram Analysis: The flow diagram helps in understanding the complexity and interdependencies of various issues. For example, issues like "Instructions Clarity" and "Packaging Problems" might be common across multiple products and suppliers. GENAI can assist in automating responses to common issues, reducing the workload on customer support teams. Furthermore, by analyzing customer feedback, GENAI can provide insights into customer sentiment, helping the support team to prioritize issues that have the most significant impact on customer satisfaction.

    5. Interacting with GPT on Vectorised Documents

Various personas can utilize this interactive widget on the SAC dashboard to pose specific queries. These queries are converted into vector format, matched against Vectorised customer reviews stored in the SAP HANA database, and finally summarized by GPT. This approach facilitates a comprehensive understanding of customer sentiments, performance optimization, campaign effectiveness, product-specific feedback, and competitive analysis, ensuring data-driven strategies across various business functions.

Scenario 1: Customer Sentiment Analysis for Product Improvement

Persona: Product manager

Objective: Understand customer feedback and sentiment for product enhancement.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive Widget Analysis: The interactive widget in the fifth tab of the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) dashboard allows the Product Manager to pose open-ended questions such as "Which is the best cream for young men?" Using GenAI, this query is translated into a vector format and matched against stored, vectorized customer reviews in the SAP HANA database. The result is a selection of relevant reviews and a summary generated by GPT, providing clear insights into customer preferences and sentiments. This enables the Product Manager to identify key areas for product improvement and innovation based on real customer feedback.

Scenario 2: Sales Performance Optimization

Persona: Sales manager

Objective: Optimize sales strategies and improve product performance.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive Widget Analysis: The sales manager can use the interactive widget to ask questions like "What are the common complaints about our top-selling products?" The Gen AI powered widget translates this query into a vector format and retrieves relevant customer reviews from the SAP HANA database. The GPT-generated summary provides a concise overview of common issues, allowing the sales manager to address these concerns proactively. This insight helps in refining sales strategies and improving customer satisfaction.

Scenario 3: Marketing Campaign Effectiveness

Persona: Marketing professional

Objective: Evaluate the effectiveness of marketing campaigns and understand customer sentiment.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive widget analysis: The Marketing Professional can utilize the interactive widget to ask questions such as "How do customers feel about our latest marketing campaign?" The Gen AI integration translates this query into a vector format and matches it with vectorized customer reviews in the SAP HANA database. The resulting summary generated by GPT provides a clear picture of customer sentiment towards the campaign. This insight helps the Marketing Professional to assess the campaign's impact and make data-driven decisions for future marketing strategies.

Scenario 4: Product-Specific Feedback Analysis

Persona: Quality assurance manager

Objective: Gather detailed feedback on specific products for quality improvement.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive Widget Analysis: The Quality assurance manager can use the interactive widget to ask specific questions like "What are the most common issues with our new skincare line?" The Gen AI powered widget translates this query into a vector format and retrieves relevant customer reviews from the SAP HANA database. The GPT-generated summary highlights the most common issues, providing the quality assurance manager with actionable insights to improve product quality and address customer concerns effectively.

Scenario 5: Competitive Analysis

Persona: Business analyst

Objective: Analyze competitor products and customer reviews for strategic planning.

Actionable Insights:

  • Interactive Widget Analysis: The business analyst can leverage the interactive widget to ask questions such as "How do our products compare to our competitors' in terms of customer satisfaction?" The Gen AI integration translates this query into a vector format and matches it with vectorized customer reviews from both the company's and competitors' products stored in the SAP HANA database. The GPT-generated summary provides a comparative analysis of customer satisfaction, helping the Business Analyst to identify strengths and weaknesses and develop strategic plans to gain a competitive edge.

    6. Customer 360

This tab showcases an Customer 360 Dashboard, integrating GenAI and analytics to provide a comprehensive view of customer interactions and sentiments. This powerful combination enables businesses to extract actionable insights, anticipate customer needs, and customize personalized experiences, driving increased customer satisfaction and loyalty.

Scenario 1: Customer Sentiment Analysis for Product Improvement

Persona: Product manager

Objective: Understand customer feedback and sentiment for product enhancement.

Scenario 2: Proactive Customer Support

Persona: Customer support agent

Objective: Provide personalized and informed customer support. The support agent uses the sentiment analysis and issue resolution data to reassure the customer about the price adjustment and highlight the product's value for money. For instance, if a customer contacts support with a query about the "BlendMaster" product, the agent can leverage the positive sentiment towards product quality and the resolved price issue to build trust and satisfaction.

Scenario 3: Targeted Marketing Campaigns

Persona: Marketing manager

Objective: Design effective marketing campaigns based on customer sentiment. Using the sentiment data, the marketing team identifies that customers appreciate the "TrimPro" product's reliability and wet trimming experience. The campaign can focus on these features to attract similar customer segments, leading to higher engagement and conversion rates.

Technical Coffee Corner

Let's explore the underlying architecture that empowers this sales strategy ecosystem. At the heart of this setup is the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine, which excels in handling high-dimensional vector data by creating a rich vector space representation for unstructured data like product reviews. When unified with the SAP Datasphere, this sophisticated engine facilitates efficient data ingestion, transformation, and harmonization, bridging the gap between isolated SAP S/4HANA transactional data and sentiment-driven insights. To enhance this further, SAP BTP plays a pivotal role in orchestrating these advanced analytics workflows, ensuring seamless data integration, real-time processing, and scalability. Leveraging the power of Large Language Models (LLM), the system employs fine-tuned AI models to contextualize and enrich product reviews, breaking them down into granular sentiment scores and categorized themes. These enriched insights are then fed into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), where the robust analytics capabilities and interactive dashboards take center stage. SAC's connectivity and integration layers enable smooth data transfers, while its advanced algorithms enable predictive and prescriptive analytics, providing a multi-dimensional view of sales metrics. The cooperation of these platforms not only elevates the data processing capabilities but also equips businesses to generate forward-looking insights. For any help or clarification, you may contact us at @DKK  , @Dimitrios_Lyras .

Conclusion

Integrating Gen AI with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud offers substantial benefits for both IT and business personas. For IT professionals, this combination simplifies the management of complex data landscapes, ensuring robust data security while enhancing the scalability of analytics tasks. With advanced analytical models in SAP Analytics Cloud, IT teams can more easily manage multiple data sources, thereby improving operational efficiency. Business users—including sales managers, marketing teams, product developers, and customer support agents—gain critical insights that drive strategic decision-making. The deep sentiment analysis enhances personalized marketing campaigns, improves customer support, and optimizes supply chain interactions. Utilizing these insights helps organizations shift from reactive to proactive strategies, significantly boosting sales performance, enhancing customer satisfaction, and increasing overall market responsiveness. This comprehensive approach ensures that both IT and business stakeholders can achieve their goals more effectively, leading to sustained business growth and a decisive competitive advantage.

entry.title: Re: 物料主数据中的批次双计量单位没有找到对应的API
entry.content:
Thank you for your question, @dingyechao .

I machine-translated it to English. 请使用您喜欢的工具翻译回中文: 

No corresponding API found for batch double unit of measure in material master
We have a customer who wants to integrate with PLM. We need to create a material master in the PLM system and then synchronize to SAP, but we found that there is no API for this field for batch dual units of measure in the SAP S4 HANA Cloud ES system, so the PLM system has no way of passing this field. Do you have a good solution? Customer code #CoE#YCMT

Let me try this for an answer: 

I may not have a good solution, but it might just work, anyway. If you are going to use the field for batch dual units of measure in S/4HANA Cloud or in a custom application you are building in embedded Steampunk a.k.a. SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment, import it into a custom field for now.

In parallel go to the SAP Customer Influence pages here at and submit a request to amend the respective API with that field, ideally with a good business case why this is a business necessity and not solvable any other way. Find other customers who might want to support you in this endeavor and up-vote your request. 

Once SAP delivers that field in the standard API, remove your mapping to custom field and use SAP's field henceforth.

If this helped address your request, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Five years of CPILint
entry.content:
On this day five years ago, I released the first version of CPILint on GitHub. I finished the latest version two days ago, so this feels like a good time to take a break, enjoy a piece of birthday cake, and look back at the project’s early days.

(The cake is a “Kagemand”, by the way, which translates literally to “Cake Man”. They’re served at birthday parties in Denmark, and they’re delicious 😋)

Version 1.0.0 came out on August 31st, 2019, but my earliest notes are from the autumn of 2018. They were jotted down between meetings during a visit to SAP in Walldorf. Seeing those notes today, I’m quite surprised how many of the early ideas eventually made their way into CPILint.

Reading the notes also reminded me that I originally used a different name: Flowmon. I think it was supposed to be a portmanteau of “iflow” and “monitor”, but it’s not a very good name, is it? I’m glad I changed it 😅

Even though I ended up writing CPILint in Java, I initially wanted to implement it in pure XSLT. I had a Schematron-like meta-programming approach in mind with a stylesheet compiling rules into another stylesheet, that would then transform an iflow’s internal XML format into compliance notifications.

I still have half a prototype, but the technique ultimately felt too limiting and I abandoned it. I still quite like the idea, though. It’s quirky and just a little chaotic 🙃 And it’s not a bad thing that it didn’t work out, by the way! On the contrary, one of the wonderful things about hobby projects is that they let you follow new ideas to interesting places and generally - in the words of Kurt Vonnegut - fart around.

The first time I showed CPILint in public was at SAP Inside Track Walldorf in January 2019. I managed to have a prototype working just in time for the event. Committing publicly to doing a live demo in front of a large group of people is quite motivating after all 🫠

SIT Walldorf was also the first time I got to show off the CPILint logo:

I was certain that someone would fairly quickly spot where the inspiration for the logo came from. However, five years later, no one has pointed out the connection. I’m a big fan of retro gaming, and the Atari 2600 console from 1977 is about as retro as it gets. This is the Asteroids game cartridge:

Look familiar? 😛 For the fifth anniversary I’ve made these logo stickers:

I’ll bring stickers with me to events in autumn, so if you’d like one, do come up and say hi!

Well, the “Kagemand” beckons, so let me wrap this up. Working on CPILint for the last five years has been so much fun, and I have no intention whatsoever of stopping here! The list of features that I want to implement is still very long, and I’m excited about bringing them to you in future releases.

Thank you for reading and thank you for supporting CPILint!

entry.title: Warning messages during transport import for "Import steps not specific to transport request"
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

 

We have noticed that long after the import was completed, the transport log was updated with a new section "Import steps not specific to transport request" that has several warning messages. It is mainly due to the mass object activation that ran in the background.

 

Can you please look into this issue and suggest us the next course of action ?

 

Thanks and Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Warning messages during transport import for "Import...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

Some import steps are not linked directly to a transport request. For example "Activate inactive runtime objects", "ABAP Dictionary Distribution" and "Feedback after export or import".

For example, during the step "Activate inactive runtime objects", the inactive nametabs are activated and there is nowhere a link to the transport that imported the DDIC change, this phase is listed under "Import steps not specific to transport request".

 

So the title "Import steps not specific to transport request" means that the system does not know to which request the steps belong to. The fact that many steps are listed in the "Import steps not specific to transport request" section of a transport request does not mean that all of them belong to this request.

When any kind of import is performed on the system (transports, Support Packages) a mass activation, distribution, conversion and movenametabs can be carried out on the system. The contents of the transport may be imported successfully but you may have outstanding conversions or activations related to objects independent of the transpor request. Until these inconsistencies are completed you will continue to see logs written under "Import steps not specific to transport" of all future transports. That is why it is written again to the transport log.

 

Kind regards.

GLStoreAmountSales%
ElectricityA10050020%

All Formulas and Calculations | SAP Help Portal

RentA1500#DIV/0!
WarehouseA2000#DIV/0!
SalesA500500100%

Can you please let us know what way we can achieve this in SAC ?

Wahid.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Double-Click Actions Not Recognized in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, I've tried double clicking in S/4 and found that I get the same result, i.e. a double is not recorded in S/4.  When I try double clicking in other applications, e.g. Word, I don't have an issue, the action is recorded correctly.  When I try recording other browser-based applications, such as Manager, double clicks are not recorded correctly.  So, I suspect that there is an issue with the browser extension or something similar.

Anyway, raise a ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and get support involved.  You will have to manually add double click actions to your Simulations at present, unless knows a work around. 

If you are concerned about the impact on content translation, use the translation capability provided through the integration to SAP Translation Hub, it also translates manually entered text.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Can not create temp file: Failed to open the connection. Database Vendor Code: 17002
entry.content:
Hi all, I'm run into an issue with a custom Spring Boot application that uses the RAS SDK. Here's what I can share:

Environments:

SAP BO

SAP BO: 4.3 SP4 which recently underwent an in-place upgrade from 4.2 SP

OS: Windows Server 2016 Datacentre

Local dev environment

OS: Windows 11 Enterprise 22H2

IDE: Intellij Ultimate 2024.2.0.2

JDK: openJDK 17.0.2

Spring Boot: 3.3.2

Crystal Reports SDK: 14.3.4.5014

Linux test environment

OS: CentOS 7

JDK: openJDK 17.0.2

Situation:

I’ve developed a Spring Boot application that:

  • Makes a connection to our CMS

  • Returns a ReportClientDocument with the provided CUID

  • Passes a single ID (Long) to a single parameter (“INVOICE_ID”) via a ParameterFieldController

  • Exports the report as a PDF to an InputStream using reportClientDocument.getPrintOutputController()

This code works as expected from my Windows 11 dev machine. The PDF version of the report is exported and downloaded to my browser.

When I run this code on the Linux environment, the application throws an exception when attempting to export to PDF.

Here’s the exception:

Can not create temp file: Failed to open the connection.
7984_16364_{1D957F0F-EE8B-4DA1-AB65-14DC86C1469F}.rpt
Details:  [Database Vendor Code: 17002 ]

Here are some code snippets:

ISessionMgr sessionMgr = CrystalEnterprise.getSessionMgr();
IEnterpriseSession enterpriseSession = sessionMgr.logon(reportsUser, reportsPassword, reportsCmsname,reportsAuthen);

ILogonTokenMgr logonTokenMgr = enterpriseSession.getLogonTokenMgr();
String logonToken = logonTokenMgr.createLogonToken("", 30, 1);  
return enterpriseSession;

//...

return reportAppFactory = (IReportAppFactory) enterpriseSession.getService("RASReportService");

//...

reportAppFactory = (IReportAppFactory) ienterpriseSession.getService("RASReportService");
//Get the reportClientDoc by CUID
IInfoStore infoStore = (IInfoStore) ienterpriseSession.getService("", "InfoStore");
IInfoObjects infoObjects = infoStore.query("Select SI_ID, SI_NAME From CI_INFOOBJECTS WHERE SI_CUID = '" + cuid + "'");
return reportAppFactory.openDocument((IInfoObject) infoObjects.get(0), 0, Locale.ENGLISH);

//...

ParameterFieldController paramController = reportClientDoc.getDataDefController().getParameterFieldController();
paramController.setCurrentValues("", "INVOICE_ID", ids.toArray());

//...
//This is where an exception is thrown when run from my Linux VM
InputStream pdfStream = reportClientDocument.getPrintOutputController().export(ReportExportFormat.PDF);

The REPORTS.CrystalReports2020ReportApplicationServer trace log (level HIGH) doesn't seem to give me much (from what I can tell). This seems to be the most useful, but I'm not sure what to do with it

ErrorLog 2024  9  1 15:52:49.816 7984 5520 (Administrator:34) (..\reporthandler.cpp:13139): CReportHandler::buildReportViewerError: CSResultException thrown.   ErrorSrc:"CRPE" FileName:"..\reporthandler.cpp" LineNum:13135 ErrorCode:756 ErrorMsg:"Failed to open the connection.

I’ve come across a few posts related to this error, but either have no solution or a solution that doesn’t solve my problem. I don’t think it’s a read/write issue related to to the SAP data and temp folders. I’ve confirmed that the SYSTEM account on my CMS Windows server has R/W to those folders. I’ve also tried using JDBC and ODBC connections with the report I’m working with, but does not make a difference.

The issue only happens from the Linux VM, so I’m guessing it’s something between that server and my CMS.

Any thoughts?

entry.content: Apologies, the code snippet was easier to read when I created the post.

entry.title: How to setup Solman monitoring and alerting on MII Schedulers?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can you please let me know how to setup alert for SAP MII scheduler jobs. The jobs are stopping frequently. There was no Solman alert setup for that. It would be really helpful if someone provide the steps to setup the Solman alert for the scheduler Jobs.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to setup Solman monitoring and alerting on MII Sc...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Currently, in System Monitoring application we only deliver following standard metrics to monitor MII in technical instance template SAP MII (all versions)

  • MII DB query response time
  • MII connector response time
  • MII service response time
  • MII transaction response time

In case you want to monitor/alert on MII schedulers, maybe Interface and Connection monitoring is more in line with your requirement.

ICMon provides metric "Number of schedulers in critical status" which can calculate the number of MII schedulers in a critical status (PENDING or STOPPED).

Please follow the guide SAP MII Channel and configure the settings.

Best regards,

Ava Wang

entry.title: 模拟物料需求计划（pMRP）
entry.content:
在我们生产计划业务中，时常会遇到产能瓶颈等，影响到对客户的交货承诺，SAP公有云ERP中的pMRP（Predictive Material and Resource Planning）功能可以帮助您基于可能的客户需求，通过模拟计划的方式，识别产能问题，并尝试找到导致产能问题的原因解决它。

计划模拟通常基于预测出的市场需求，在系统中，我们以独立需求的形式创建预测数量

然后去对您需要模拟的一个或者一组物料创建模拟计划

评估和处理pMRP模拟结果

 如果模拟结果是有产能超复合，或者物料货源问题（如没有生产版本等），在Demand Plan Simulation界面你可以看到各种问题的数量。

对于未找到生产版本的物料，创建好生产版本再重新scheduling pMRP simulation后错误消息会消失。

对于产能的问题，可以通过Issue list查看受影响的工作中心，以及超负荷的情况：

对于这些产能超负荷的工作中心，系统给出来了调整建议，比如将需求提前到有产能剩余的工作周，您可以采用这些建议，直接对需求数量和日期进行调整。

在Simulation Summary界面可以查看所有做过的变更，或者忽略的变更建议：

这些变更会在Release后对独立需求生效，也就是独立需求的数量和日期会在模拟计划Release后进行相应的变更。

这样，你就通过模拟计划实现了提前了解产品相关资源供应情况，在资源不足的情况下及时对生产资源或者需求计划做出调整，以更好的应对市场需求变化。

————————————————————————————————————————————
**本博文中涉及到的截图均来源于SAP内部测试环境。

entry.title: Re: Issue with Accessing Simulation in Published Version
entry.content:
Hi,

The published version of the Simulation URL should contain either /pub/ or /~tag/published/ embedded in the URL. For example:

https://anzdemo1.enable-now.cloud.sap/pub/Adoption/index.html?library=library.txt&show=project!PR_A2C5281E809B4D8D

https://anzdemo1.enable-now.cloud.sap/wa/Adoption/~tag/published/index.html?show=project!PR_7D5803DE0B07FC8C

If the URL that is causing the issue does not contain either string, it is the wrong version of the URL, i.e. the version for authors to check the content before publishing.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Enabling Web Assistant
entry.content:
Hi Aussie_Ray,

If the SAP Companion editing pencil button is not displayed at all, try embedding the following string into the URL of the application that contains SAP Companion, directly following the? in the URL:

help-editor=true&

For example, I can turn on the editing pencil in S/4HANA by amending the URL to look something like:

https://my304870.s4hana.ondemand.com/ui?help-editor=true#Shell-home

I didn't put the & after help-editor=true because there were no other parameter in the URL, the & is used as a separator if existing parameters are already in the URL directly following the ?

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: CKR1 timing issue
entry.content:
OK so it seems that I have solved the problem.

The reason this was only affecting myself on site is that I am the only one who would run t-code s_alr_87099932 on the first day of the month to validate the differences in the previous costings and the current ones.

Running this t-code however copied the Costing Run name and the Costing Date into memory and then auto-populating it in CKR1 in fields that are not immediately viewable when you use this t-code.   They are viewable once the 'All Selections' button is clicked.

Once these values were removed it all worked as it should.

entry.title: Re: Need help to get Build-Apps ressources
entry.content: As far as I know, the default table view is read-only and not really customizable. You'd have to build your own table, not very difficult. I don't know what is the best way to handle changes, but I imagine at the very least you can add a button for every row to update that record, or you could have a single button to update all the data (checking first which rows changed). You could use a change event on each component and every click will return an event at which point you could save or just indicate that the value has changed ... depends how you want the UI to work.

entry.title: Downtime-Optimized Conversion(Doc) for SAP S/4HANA - Finance (Functional)
entry.content:
This blog is intended for project members responsible for Doc Conversion functionality in S4 Hana Finance. It explains the Standard options for Doc Conversion functionality and Data migration. Consultants who have Standard conversion Experience can understand the functionality very easily. This blog is based on the real time Project experience which gone live recently.

What Is DOC:

The approach is based on the idea to move downtime activities (like data conversion from old to new data model) and database migration (in case of source system on non-HANA database) to uptime processing of SUM. For a standard conversion, the FIN data conversion has to be executed after the SUM has finished, by respective IMG activities (e.g. FIN customizing, FIN data migration). The downtime-optimized approach will even move the FIN data migration (partly) into the SUM uptime processing.

Data conversion is moved to uptime processing. If the source system is not yet on SAP HANA database, the database migration of the affected tables is then done in uptime as well, prior to this data conversion.

Overview:

  1. The approach can be used for a system conversion from SAP ERP 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA.
  2. It reduces the technical downtime by executing data conversion and migration (if required) in uptime.
  3. Approach is possible for source systems on SAP HANA or on non - SAP HANA database.
  4. Approach will require additional effort for project planning, impact analysis, and additional test runs.

Planning for Downtime Optimized Conversion 

During DOC conversion we must follow below restrictions or Freeze

  1. Stack Freeze: No changes to Stack on source ECC​, means we are not going to upgrade /Update any stack in source system .
  2. FI Customizing Freeze: Customizing Freeze initiates a phase during which Customizing changes as well as many Finance and Controlling activities are no longer possible. The Customizing Freeze starts once the production system is copied to the sandbox to prepare the final Finance Customizing transport.

No (customizing) changes allowed; For details check downtime optimized Conversion Guide attached to Note 2443938. A “standard” SAP S/4HANA conversion is a prerequisite, this freeze is between standard and Doc to keep consistency​.

Please Find the below link where you can find the customization restriction for finance.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/a882504353b1407da13a271cf9dfcaf5/1f04d85a0fb7420c954d2365634ec883.html

3. Hard Freeze : Reoccurs during every downtime-optimized Conversion iteration.

Following are list of transactions that are locked by SUM during the short FI-AA freeze:​

  • KA12 – Archive cost centers (plan)​
  • KA16 – Archive cost centers (line items)​
  • AS91 – Legacy data transfer​
  • AJRW – FI-AA Balance Carryforward​
  • AJAB – FI-AA Year End Close
  • SARA – Data Archiving Administration​
    •  

Duration ​

Starts with phase “lock workbench” in SUM preprocessing and ends after the completion of the creation of the clone for downtime simulation (valid for Load Verification and Dress Rehearsal) or the completion of the Productive Cutover (valid for Go Live)

4.Recording Freeze: 

  • Avoid Full Retirements or Full Retiring Transfers (transactions which deactivate assets w depreciation). Do not post full retirements or full retiring transfers starting the first day of the open period when triggers are activated.
  • Changes in Material Ledger are not allowed​. Activation or deactivation of Material Ledger Actual Costing for one or more plants​. Use of transaction CKMSTART (Production Startup of Material Ledger). ​Initialization of periods for material master records (MMPI)​​

 Active Material Ledger Actual Costing before SAP S/4HANA conversion​. The following must be completed in the source system before executing the final delta migration run ​

  • Complete all Material Ledger costing runs for actual costing (transaction CKMLCP) ​
  • Complete all alternative valuation runs (transaction CKMLCPAVR)​
  • After the system conversion, no changes to costing runs and alternative valuation runs created before the system conversion are possible​.

The following changes in Inventory Management are not allowed​

  • Archiving for materials (archiving object MM_MATBEL)​
  • Archiving for material documents (archiving object MM_MATBEL)​
  • Execute MM-IM inconsistency check reports (note 1227770) to correct MM-IM data
  • ​Corrections are recorded in table MMINKON_UP

5.FI-AA Closing Freeze: Before the beginning of the creation of the triggers and ends after the successful activation of the trigger recording

  •  Avoid FI-AA <-> FI-GL mismatches during the FI-AA data conversion​
  • Execute consistent FI-AA closing activities (RAPOST2000, RAPERB2000, AFAR, …)​
  • Avoid any intermediate FI-AA postings to ensure consistent data for initial FI data conversion​.

Transports to be considered: Any functional transports created in ECC as part of Pre-conversion before SUM tool (By default SUM will consider these transports as part of CTI) and Post Conversion Transports before the Data Migration will be part of DOC cycle.

Below Configuration nodes for which TR created for Standard conversion are part of CTI Buffer for Doc conversion.

  • Check Customizing Settings Prior to Migration
  • Set Number of Jobs for Activities in Mass Data Framework
  • Preparations and Migration of Customizing for General Ledger
  • Preparations and Migration of Customizing for Accrual Engine
  • Preparations and Migration of Customizing for Asset Accounting
  • Preparations and Migration of Customizing for Controlling
  • Preparations and Migration of Customizing for Material Ledger
  • Preparations for Migration of House Bank Accounts
  • Preparations for Migration of Financial Documents to Trade Finance
  • Preparatory Activities and Migration of Customizing for Credit Management
  • Transport Accepted Error Messages

Note :

  1. Transport created for the node Activities after Migration is not part of CTI Buffer.
  2. Number of jobs mentioned in the node Set Number of Jobs for Activities in Mass Data Framework will be the same jobs in Doc conversion. You can’t change the no of Jobs in Doc Cycle. please make sure you maintained the correct number of jobs and capture in the transport.
  3. Make sure you capture the activation of new asset accounting mentioned in the node Activate Asset Accounting (New)
  4. Make sure you capture the accepted errors during Data migration into a transport mentioned in node Transport Accepted Error Messages
  5. During Doc Conversion if you created any new plant for which we have not captured the TR for ML activation, please use transaction code UPG_SFIN_CUST before start of data migration.

Data Migration:

  • Data migration divided into two parts. Historical Data which is part of Standard cycle and Delta Data after the trigger. During Doc Cycle Historical data will automatically Update during Up-time and Delta data will update during Down-time.
  • The Errors which were accepted during data migration in Standard cycle which is captured in the transport will automatically accept in Doc Conversion cycle.
  • If any new errors come up during Doc Conversion cycle must accepted manually with the help of Doc Basis consultant.
  • Functional consultant will take help from the Basis team to accept the error message because we need to login DDIC users in the shadow system. generally, we get one DDIC user id for the conversion.

Credit Management Migration:

  • We must perform Credit Management Migration similar as standard conversion in the Doc Cycle.

Complete Migration:

  • We must Set Migration to Completed similar as standard conversion in the Doc Cycle after the credit management migration.

Activities after Migration:

  • We must perform all activities mentioned in the node Activities after Migration similar as standard conversion.

Remove Freeze Period:

  • Once all activities completed and after getting confirmation from the Basis team, Business can create transports and send to other systems and also business can change the master data .

Relevant Notes:

  • Guide for downtime-optimized Conversion with SUM 2.0 SP 17
  • SAP Note 3301507 on downtime-optimized Conversion with SUM 2.0 SP 17
  • SAP Note 2879083 Downtime-optimized Procedures using downtime-optimized Conversion: additional information on data replication
  • SAP Note 2402270 Export of Table Statistics for SUM Impact Analysis
  • SAP Note 2481983 SUM Impact Analysis for downtime-optimized DMO / downtime-optimized Conversion

Note :

  • For standard Conversion, we crate transport requests for Post conversion activity in the Dev system and these TRs are moved to Production but for Doc conversion we have to create TRs for Every cycle ie Development, quality and pre-Production.
  • Transport Requests created in standard Conversion cycle will be the part of CTI buffer for Doc Conversion
  • For every Doc Conversion cycle, standard Conversion is mandatory.
  • If we missed any Freeze mentioned above, we have to start from the scratch for every cycle.
  • Doc Conversion is best for the customer which have more transaction data and they need less down time.
  • Load Verification Cycle is required for Real production load and recorded data changes just before Production cycle.
  • Make sure month end, quarter end and Year end activities are completed before starting Doc Cycles

Conclusion:

This blog is very useful for all the customers and Consultants who are planning to implement Doc in the S4 system .it Covers the Consideration of Understand the supported various business scenarios.
